description: A municipal utility board in Arizona needs a Levelized Cost of Energy (LCOE) estimate for a proposed 100 MW utility-scale solar plant near Tucson. Provide a Python script at /home/ga/Documents/SAM_Projects/compute_lcoe.py and a resulting JSON file at /home/ga/Documents/SAM_Projects/utility_pv_lcoe_result.json based on a PySAM PVWatts and LCOE financial simulation. Use an Arizona TMY weather file from the directory path in /home/ga/.SAM/solar_resource_dir.txt. The simulation must use a 100,000 kW DC system capacity, 1.3 DC-to-AC ratio, single-axis tracking (array type 2), 0 degree tilt, 180 degree azimuth, 14% system losses, and standard module type (0). Financial parameters for the Fixed Charge Rate method include a $100,000,000 capital cost, $1,500,000 fixed annual O&M cost, $0 variable O&M cost, and a 0.0713 fixed charge rate. The JSON output must contain exactly these keys: annual_energy_kwh (numeric first-year energy), capacity_factor_pct (numeric), lcoe_cents_per_kwh (numeric real LCOE), and weather_file_used (string basename).
